### 1. `mcp-connection-session-resilience` тАФ Enrich server_info with connection readiness and add heartbeat

**Status:** merged (PR #218, 2026-04-17) ┬╖ **Order:** 1 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-correctness ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 45000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Fixed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `mcp-connection-session-resilience` |
| Diagnosis | Consumer repos report `Not connected` for `user-roslyn` / `roslyn` and fall back to Grep/Bash, bypassing the policy. `server_info` today returns version + update hints but no explicit `connectionState` / `workspacesLoadedCount` signal (verified in `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/ServerTools.cs` тАФ the tool response has no readiness/connection field). v1.18.2 closed the plugin-loader REQUIRED-mode config crash; the broader resilience slice remains open. |
| Approach | (a) Add a `connection` subfield to `server_info` with `{ state: "ready"\|"initializing"\|"degraded", loadedWorkspaceCount, stdioPid, serverStartedAt }` emitted from `ServerTools` using `WorkspaceManager` counts. (b) Add a `server_heartbeat` stable tool that returns the same shape without the full version payload (cheaper poll). (c) Document the `workspace_load` ordering in `ai_docs/runtime.md` so consumers know to call it before any workspace-scoped tool. |
| Scope | prod: 2тАУ3 (`src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/ServerTools.cs`, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Catalog/ServerSurfaceCatalog.cs` to register `server_heartbeat`, `ai_docs/runtime.md` doc update). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ServerHeartbeatTests.cs`). |
| Risks | `server_info` is a hot path for plugin startup тАФ must not regress payload size; keep `connection` small (<300 bytes). Parity test in `SurfaceCatalogTests` will require the new `server_heartbeat` entry. |
| Validation | New tests assert heartbeat returns `ready` after `workspace_load`, `initializing` before any load, and includes the `loadedWorkspaceCount`. Manual: run stdio host, `server_heartbeat` before and after `workspace_load`. |
| Performance review | N/A тАФ correctness/resilience fix, no hot-path changes. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `server_heartbeat` stable tool and `connection` subfield on `server_info` so consumers can distinguish transport-ready vs. workspace-loaded states (`mcp-connection-session-resilience`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `mcp-connection-session-resilience`. |

### 2. `mcp-server-surface-catalog-parity-generator` тАФ Promote parity test to build-time analyzer

**Status:** merged (PR #220, 2026-04-17) ┬╖ **Order:** 2 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-correctness ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 50000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Fixed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `mcp-server-surface-catalog-parity-generator` |
| Diagnosis | Drift between `[McpServerTool]` / `[McpServerResource]` / `[McpServerPrompt]` decorated methods and the hand-maintained `ServerSurfaceCatalog.Tools/Resources/Prompts` lists (`src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Catalog/ServerSurfaceCatalog.cs:11,171,188`) is only caught at test-run by `SurfaceCatalogTests.ServerSurfaceCatalog_CoversAllRegisteredToolsResourcesAndPrompts`. PR #188 validated this тАФ I-21 added three `[McpServerResource]` attributes and build-green тЖТ test-red was the first signal. |
| Approach | Ship a Roslyn analyzer (new `.csproj` under `analyzers/ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzer`) that (a) enumerates methods decorated with the three attributes at compile time, (b) reads the hand-maintained lists' initializers, (c) emits `RMCP001: SurfaceCatalog missing entry for <name>` / `RMCP002: SurfaceCatalog entry not backed by attribute`. Wire the analyzer into the host csproj via `<ProjectReference OutputItemType="Analyzer">`. Keep the runtime parity test as a belt-and-braces assertion for now. |
| Scope | prod: 3 (new `analyzers/ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzer/ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzer.cs`, new `analyzers/ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzer/ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzer.csproj`, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio.csproj` to wire). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzerTests.cs` hosting AnalyzerVerifier<T> cases). |
| Risks | Analyzer must load cleanly under the repo's `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true`; crashing analyzers block all builds. Needs `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Analyzers` reference. Analyzer package must ship as a roslyn-analyzer package, not as code in the host. |
| Validation | Tests: AnalyzerVerifier cases for (a) attributed-but-missing, (b) listed-but-not-attributed, (c) all-matching clean path. Manual: remove a `Resource(тАж)` line, confirm `RMCP002`; add a new `[McpServerResource]` without catalog, confirm `RMCP001`. |
| Performance review | Analyzers run every build; keep the symbol walk scoped to `Host.Stdio` assembly only. No per-keystroke IDE cost beyond standard Roslyn analyzer overhead. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `ServerSurfaceCatalogAnalyzer` emits `RMCP001`/`RMCP002` at build time when decorated MCP methods drift from the hand-maintained `ServerSurfaceCatalog` lists (`mcp-server-surface-catalog-parity-generator`). Replaces a test-run-time-only parity check. |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `mcp-server-surface-catalog-parity-generator`. |

### 3. `scaffold-test-sibling-pattern-inference` тАФ Infer integration-fixture shape from sibling tests

**Status:** merged (PR #238, 2026-04-17) ┬╖ **Order:** 3 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-UX ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 40000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `scaffold-test-sibling-pattern-inference` |
| Diagnosis | `scaffold_test_preview` in `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ScaffoldingService.cs` emits a generic xUnit stub with no detection of sibling-test conventions (ASP.NET Core `IClassFixture<CustomWebApplicationFactory>`, `[Trait("Category","Integration")]`, `_client`, constructor-injected factory). Firewall-analyzer session 2026-04-15 rewrote the stub by hand as a result. |
| Approach | Add an optional `referenceTestFile` parameter to `scaffold_test_preview`. When provided (or auto-detected as the most-recently-modified `*Tests.cs` in the target project), inspect the sibling's class declaration, attributes, base class, and constructor injections, and replicate them on the new stub. Detection is text + Roslyn symbol walk; no network / MSBuild changes. |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ScaffoldingService.cs`, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/ScaffoldingTools.cs` for the new parameter surface). tests: +1 (extend `tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ScaffoldingIntegrationTests.cs` with a new ASP.NET Core fixture case). |
| Risks | Auto-detection must not pick a non-test file; must resolve to the project's test project, not the production project. Interface changes to ScaffoldingTools break existing callers тАФ new parameter must be optional with sensible default. |
| Validation | Integration test creates a fixture project with `IClassFixture<TestFactory>` pattern and confirms scaffold output mirrors the attribute + constructor shape. Manual: run against an ASP.NET Core repo. |
| Performance review | N/A тАФ non-hot path (scaffolding is a write-heavy, once-per-task operation). |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `scaffold_test_preview` accepts an optional `referenceTestFile` and replicates sibling-test scaffolding (fixture class, `IClassFixture<T>`, attributes) when a pattern is inferable (`scaffold-test-sibling-pattern-inference`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `scaffold-test-sibling-pattern-inference`. |

### 4. `exception-handler-classification-tracer` тАФ trace_exception_flow tool

**Status:** merged (PR #239, 2026-04-17) ┬╖ **Order:** 4 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-UX ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 50000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `exception-handler-classification-tracer` |
| Diagnosis | Error-handling refactors (5 of 13 initiatives in PR sweep #182тАУ#188) required mapping exception-type X to every catch-site + its envelope. `find_references` returns usage sites not handling sites. `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/ToolErrorHandler.cs:1` confirms the centralized classifier that each session traces manually. |
| Approach | New `IExceptionFlowService.TraceExceptionFlowAsync` + `trace_exception_flow(workspaceId, exceptionTypeMetadataName, scopeProjectFilter?)` tool. Walks every compilation unit for `CatchClauseSyntax`, resolves declared exception type via the semantic model, and reports (filePath, line, containingMethod, first 200 chars of body, reThrowAsTypeMetadataName) for catches where the declared type is assignable from the target (or equals it). |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/ExceptionFlowService.cs` new, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/ExceptionFlowTools.cs` new тАФ plus one-line catalog entry in `ServerSurfaceCatalog.cs` if the analyzer from #2 has not shipped yet). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ExceptionFlowServiceTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Catches with pattern-matched `when` clauses need the body excerpt to capture the filter condition so the agent sees what actually matches. Must also handle rethrow-as-different-type (`throw new X()` inside the catch) to flag translations. |
| Validation | Tests on the repo's own `ToolErrorHandler` catches: trace `JsonException` тЖТ finds `ClassifyError`'s InvocationWrapper guard (I-16 root cause) and the PromptShim wrap site (I-16's fix). |
| Performance review | Per-workspace syntax-tree walk; O(n catch clauses). Bounded payload via optional `maxResults`. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `trace_exception_flow` returns every `catch` clause whose declared type is assignable from the input, with body excerpt and rethrow-as annotations тАФ built to shorten error-classification refactor discovery (`exception-handler-classification-tracer`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `exception-handler-classification-tracer`. |

### 5. `composite-split-service-di-registration` тАФ Composite preview with DI registration deltas

**Status:** deferred (subagent stalled mid-work; branch `remediation/composite-split-service-di-registration` preserved тАФ see state.json notes) ┬╖ **Order:** 5 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-UX ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 50000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `composite-split-service-di-registration` |
| Diagnosis | Splitting one DI-consumed type into several + a forwarding facade is many disconnected edits today. `symbol_refactor_preview` landed in v1.18 and covers restructure+edit ops; `SymbolRefactorService` in `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/SymbolRefactorService.cs` is the natural host. |
| Approach | Add a composite preset `split_service_with_di_preview(workspaceId, sourceType, partitions[], hostRegistrationFile)` on top of `symbol_refactor_preview`. Uses `move_type_to_file_preview` per partition, generates a forwarding facade that delegates to each impl, and emits `preview_multi_file_edit` deltas against the host's `Add*` lines (Transient / Singleton inference from the existing registration). |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/SymbolRefactorService.cs` extend, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/SymbolRefactorTools.cs` new preset). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/CompositeSplitServiceDiPreviewTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Inferring lifetime from the existing registration call must handle both `services.AddTransient<IFoo, Foo>()` and `services.AddTransient<IFoo>(sp => new Foo(sp))` shapes. Missing host registration should fall back to a warning, not crash. |
| Validation | Unit test: a small service with two public methods splits cleanly; facade compiles; `services.AddTransient<IFoo, Foo>()` line is rewritten to `services.AddTransient<IFooFacade, FooFacade>()` + `services.AddTransient<IFooA, FooA>()` + `services.AddTransient<IFooB, FooB>()`. |
| Performance review | N/A тАФ non-hot path. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `split_service_with_di_preview` composite uses `symbol_refactor_preview` primitives to split a service, generate a forwarding facade, and emit DI-registration deltas in one preview token (`composite-split-service-di-registration`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `composite-split-service-di-registration`. |

### 6. `duplicate-method-body-detector` тАФ find_duplicated_methods

**Status:** merged (PR #245, 2026-04-18) ┬╖ **Order:** 6 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-UX ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 55000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `duplicate-method-body-detector` |
| Diagnosis | Detect near-duplicate method bodies by AST-normalized hash тАФ broader than the P4 `duplicate-helper-detection-across-modules` (which matches against referenced-package symbols). The 2026-04-15 deep-review audit spent 15 min manually finding `BuildMinimalDiff` / `BuildMinimalUnifiedDiff` / `BuildUnifiedDiff` / `BuildUnifiedDiff` duplicates across `ChangeSignatureService`, `RestructureService`, `StringLiteralReplaceService`, `EditService` (all four files exist тАФ verified). |
| Approach | New `find_duplicated_methods(workspaceId, minLines=10, similarityThreshold=0.85, projectFilter?)` extending `UnusedCodeAnalyzer` infrastructure. AST-normalize each method body (strip trivia, rename locals, compare `SyntaxKind` sequence), bucket by normalized hash, group matches, return `DuplicatedMethodGroup[]`. |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/DuplicateMethodDetectorService.cs` new, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/AdvancedAnalysisTools.cs` extend). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/DuplicateMethodDetectorTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Must not false-positive on overloads (same name, different signatures). Similarity threshold needs tuning тАФ below 0.85 risks noise; above 0.95 misses near-duplicates with minor variations. |
| Validation | Run on the current repo's `Build*Diff` helpers and confirm they cluster together. Run on a trivially-distinct set and confirm zero matches. |
| Performance review | O(n) method bodies per project; AST normalization is O(size of body). For large solutions, expose `projectFilter` to scope the scan. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `find_duplicated_methods` returns method-body clusters by AST-normalized hash across a workspace (or project scope) for internal-copy-paste discovery (`duplicate-method-body-detector`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `duplicate-method-body-detector`. |

### 7. `change-type-namespace-preview` тАФ Same-project namespace relocation

**Status:** merged (PR #244, 2026-04-18) ┬╖ **Order:** 7 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-UX ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 55000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Diagnosis | None of `move_type_to_file_preview` / `move_file_preview` / `move_type_to_project_preview` covers same-project-different-namespace. Canonical namespace-cycle fix requires 1 `Write` + 1 delete + 5 `Edit` today (DotNet-Network-Documentation F1). `TypeMoveService.cs` and `CrossProjectRefactoringService.cs` have overlapping primitives this can stitch. |
| Approach | New `change_type_namespace_preview(typeName, fromNamespace, toNamespace, newFilePath?)` service. (a) Rewrite the type's `namespace` declaration, (b) optionally relocate the file, (c) scan consumers via `find_consumers` + namespace-import diff: add new `using` where needed, remove old where sole-purpose, respect ambient parent-namespace resolution. Pair with `get_namespace_dependencies` for a suggestтЖТpreviewтЖТapplyтЖТverify-cycle-closed loop. |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/NamespaceRelocationService.cs` new, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/TypeMoveTools.cs` extend). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/NamespaceRelocationTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Ambient resolution is subtle тАФ must not drop `using`s from consumers inside the old parent namespace. File relocation path must respect workspace-root sandbox. |
| Validation | Test: break a deliberate namespace cycle by moving `DevicePlatform` from `Child.Sub` to `Child`; confirm only consumers outside the parent namespace get a new `using` and old `using`s are removed where sole-purpose. |
| Performance review | N/A тАФ non-hot path. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `change_type_namespace_preview` relocates a type between namespaces in the same project тАФ rewrites namespace declaration, optional file move, adjusts consumer `using`s respecting ambient resolution (`change-type-namespace-preview`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `change-type-namespace-preview`. |

Update the four most-run skills (`analyze`, `review`, `complexity`, `test-coverage`) with the precheck. Subsequent passes extend to the rest. |

### 20. `dr-9-12-posttooluse-hook-adds-n-1-verification-step-per` тАФ PostToolUse hook N+1 nag

**Status:** merged (PR #237, 2026-04-17) ┬╖ **Order:** 20 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P4 ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 40000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Changed тАФ BREAKING

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `dr-9-12-posttooluse-hook-adds-n-1-verification-step-per` |
| Diagnosis | `hooks/hooks.json:25` reminds the agent to `compile_check` / `build_workspace` after every `*_apply`. For N back-to-back applies, this is N verification nags тАФ noisy. |
| Approach | Either (a) replace the per-apply reminder with a *batch* reminder that fires only when the PostToolUse immediate sequence breaks (e.g. when the next tool is NOT another `*_apply`); or (b) let the hook fire but require the agent's response be appended to a single "verification pending" marker that the next non-apply tool call clears. Simplest shippable fix: change the hook prompt to "if another *_apply is likely to follow immediately, respond 'ok'; otherwise remind." |
| Scope | prod: 1 (`hooks/hooks.json`). tests: 0 (hook behavior is prompt-driven). |
| Risks | If the heuristic is too permissive, verification may be genuinely missed; keep the batch-close tolerant. This is a BEHAVIOR change, hence Changed тАФ BREAKING label even though no public API shifts. |
| Validation | Manual: chain 3 `*_apply` calls followed by `compile_check`; the PostToolUse hook should collapse into one reminder (or zero reminders if the `compile_check` itself follows). |
| Performance review | N/A. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Changed тАФ BREAKING:** PostToolUse `*_apply` verification reminder batches across back-to-back applies; emits a single reminder when the sequence breaks rather than one per apply (`dr-9-12-posttooluse-hook-adds-n-1-verification-step-per`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `dr-9-12-posttooluse-hook-adds-n-1-verification-step-per`. |

### 21. `lcom4-lifecycle-pattern-false-positive` тАФ Lifecycle-pattern detection in cohesion metrics

**Status:** merged (PR #248, 2026-04-18) ┬╖ **Order:** 21 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P4 ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 40000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Fixed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `lcom4-lifecycle-pattern-false-positive` |
| Diagnosis | `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/CohesionAnalysisService.cs` (406 lines) reports LCOM4 тЙе 3 for lifecycle-pattern action/handler types whose methods are orthogonal on fields by design (`Describe`/`Validate`/`Execute` triad). Every audit session manually triages these. |
| Approach | Add a lifecycle-pattern detector to `CohesionAnalysisService`: detect types whose name ends in `Action`/`Handler`/`Command`/`Stage` AND implement a `Describe` + `Validate*` + `Execute*` triad (or similar well-known lifecycle shape). Emit a new field `lifecyclePattern: "action-triad" | null` in `CohesionMetricsDto` and downgrade the LCOM4 recommendation string when detected. |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/CohesionAnalysisService.cs`, `src/RoslynMcp.Core/Models/CohesionMetricsDto.cs` to add the new field). tests: +1 (extend `tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/CohesionAnalysisTests.cs` with action/handler fixtures). |
| Risks | False-negatives (real low cohesion that happens to match the lifecycle shape). Detector should be conservative: require the exact triad + naming convention, not either/or. |
| Validation | Test: `FooAction { Describe, Validate, Execute }` classifies as action-triad with LCOM4 suppressed message; `FooAction { Describe, Foo, Bar }` still reports LCOM4=3 with default message. |
| Performance review | Pattern check is O(1) per type; no hot-path impact. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Fixed:** `get_cohesion_metrics` recognizes the action/handler lifecycle triad (`Describe`/`Validate*`/`Execute*`) and emits `lifecyclePattern: "action-triad"`, downgrading the LCOM4 split recommendation for types that are orthogonal on fields by design (`lcom4-lifecycle-pattern-false-positive`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `lcom4-lifecycle-pattern-false-positive`. |

| Approach | New `find_duplicate_helpers(workspaceId, projectFilter?)` that flags private/internal extension methods whose body-shape (single pattern-match / null-check / switch on well-known BCL type) duplicates a reachable symbol from a referenced package. Reuse `UnusedCodeAnalyzer` symbol-walking infrastructure. |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/UnusedCodeAnalyzer.cs` extend with new heuristic category, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/AdvancedAnalysisTools.cs` extend). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/DuplicateHelperDetectionTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Must not false-positive on legitimate domain-specific wrappers. Conservative: only flag when body is structurally тЙд 2 statements AND the canonical target is a single method call on a BCL / NuGet-referenced symbol. |
| Validation | Test fixture: local `StringHelper.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s) => string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(s)` тЖТ detected; `StringHelper.NormalizeForMyDomain(s)` тЖТ not detected. |
| Performance review | O(n extension methods) per project; bounded. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `find_duplicate_helpers` flags private/internal extension-method helpers whose body shape duplicates a reachable BCL/NuGet symbol (`duplicate-helper-detection-across-modules`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `duplicate-helper-detection-across-modules`. |

### 26. `dr-9-10-initial-does-not-wait-for-concurrent-to-finaliz` тАФ workspace_load awaits concurrent dotnet restore

**Status:** merged (PR #262, 2026-04-18) ┬╖ **Order:** 26 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P4 ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 50000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Fixed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `dr-9-10-initial-does-not-wait-for-concurrent-to-finaliz` |
| Diagnosis | `workspace_load` can race a concurrent out-of-process `dotnet restore`: the workspace loads against an in-flight `obj/project.assets.json`, causing stale `MetadataReference` handles and the CS1705 drift documented in `ai_docs/runtime.md` Known issues. |
| Approach | Add a best-effort wait in `WorkspaceManager.LoadAsync`: probe for `obj/*.dgspec.json` or `obj/project.assets.json` mtime stability (~250ms repeatable stable check, 2s cap) before handing the MSBuildWorkspace snapshot to callers. Gated behind `ROSLYNMCP_RESTORE_RACE_WAIT_MS` env var (default 2000ms, 0 disables). |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/WorkspaceManager.cs` тАФ 898 lines, add a small wait helper, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Program.cs` env-var read). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/WorkspaceLoadRestoreRaceTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Wait must not block a workspace-load when no restore is in flight (detect via `.dgspec.json` absence / stable mtime). 2s cap prevents a stuck build from blocking startup. |
| Validation | Test: simulate in-flight restore by touching `project.assets.json` during load; confirm loader waits until mtime stabilizes. |
| Performance review | Adds up to 2s only when restore is actually in flight; no-op otherwise. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Fixed:** `workspace_load` waits (up to `ROSLYNMCP_RESTORE_RACE_WAIT_MS`, default 2000ms) for a concurrent `dotnet restore` to produce stable `obj/project.assets.json` mtimes before snapshotting тАФ eliminates spurious CS1705 drift seen in samplesolution audit (`dr-9-10-initial-does-not-wait-for-concurrent-to-finaliz`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `dr-9-10-initial-does-not-wait-for-concurrent-to-finaliz`. |

### 27. `semantic-edit-with-compile-check-wrapper` тАФ apply_text_edit verify mode

**Status:** merged (PR #260, 2026-04-18) ┬╖ **Order:** 27 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P4 ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 50000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `semantic-edit-with-compile-check-wrapper` |
| Diagnosis | `apply_text_edit` (in `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/EditTools.cs`, verified 35 lines total) has no verify mode. Sessions run separate `compile_check` calls after each `Edit`. `apply_with_verify` exists but requires a preview token from a semantic refactor, not raw text edits. |
| Approach | Add `verify: bool = false` + `autoRevertOnError: bool = false` parameters to `apply_text_edit`. When `verify=true`, run `compile_check` scoped to the owning project after the edit; return both the edit diff and the diagnostics. When `autoRevertOnError=true` AND new errors appeared, revert via the existing `revert_last_apply` single-slot. Also add to `apply_multi_file_edit` for symmetry. |
| Scope | prod: 3 (`src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/EditTools.cs`, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/MultiFileEditTools.cs`, `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/EditService.cs` if verify dispatch flows through there). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ApplyTextEditVerifyTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Auto-revert must be single-shot per call (don't revert prior-turn edits). Must not auto-revert when errors pre-exist unrelated to the edit тАФ compare pre- and post-edit diagnostic sets. |
| Validation | Test: verify edit that introduces a syntax error + `autoRevertOnError=true` тЖТ edit is rolled back, diagnostics returned. Verify edit that introduces no errors тЖТ normal path, diagnostics clean. |
| Performance review | `compile_check` on the owning project only; no hotspot impact. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `apply_text_edit` / `apply_multi_file_edit` accept `verify=true` + `autoRevertOnError=true` тАФ wraps the edit with `compile_check` on the owning project and optionally rolls back on new errors (`semantic-edit-with-compile-check-wrapper`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `semantic-edit-with-compile-check-wrapper`. |

### 28. `workspace-stale-after-external-edit-feedback` тАФ FileSystemWatcher-driven isStale

**Status:** merged (PR #267, 2026-04-18) ┬╖ **Order:** 28 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P4 ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 50000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `workspace-stale-after-external-edit-feedback` |
| Diagnosis | `workspace_status verbose=false` returns `isStale` but doesn't detect edits made by Claude Code's `Edit` tool outside the server's change tracker. `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/FileWatcherService.cs` exists тАФ extend it. |
| Approach | Extend `FileWatcherService` to mark `WorkspaceManager.IsStale = true` when a tracked `.cs`/`.csproj`/`.slnx` file changes outside the server's apply-channel. Add a `staleReason: "external-edit"` / `"apply"` / `"restore"` subfield to `WorkspaceStatusDto` for observability. Make `change_signature_preview` and other write-preview tools refuse with a specific error envelope when `isStale=true`, pointing at `workspace_reload`. |
| Scope | prod: 3 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/FileWatcherService.cs`, `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/WorkspaceManager.cs`, `src/RoslynMcp.Core/Models/WorkspaceStatusDto.cs`). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/ExternalEditStalenessTests.cs`). |
| Risks | FileSystemWatcher can miss rapid-fire batched edits; couple with a periodic mtime-poll fallback. Must not churn CPU when many `.cs` files change (e.g. during `dotnet restore`). |
| Validation | Test: load workspace тЖТ write to a tracked `.cs` via `System.IO.File` (simulating external `Edit`) тЖТ `workspace_status` reports `isStale=true`, `staleReason="external-edit"`; `change_signature_preview` refuses with a reload hint. |
| Performance review | FileSystemWatcher is low-cost; avoid rescanning entire workspace on each event тАФ just flip the flag. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** External `.cs`/`.csproj`/`.slnx` edits outside the server's change tracker flip `WorkspaceManager.IsStale` via FileSystemWatcher; `workspace_status` includes a `staleReason` field and write-preview tools refuse with a reload hint (`workspace-stale-after-external-edit-feedback`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `workspace-stale-after-external-edit-feedback`. |

| Validation | Test: method with `var x = 5` + no read тЖТ flagged; method with `var x = 5; Console.Write(x)` тЖТ not flagged; method with `out var x` passed to a caller тЖТ not flagged (the caller reads via the out). |

### 30. `pragma-directive-scope-manipulation` тАФ pragma_scope_widen / verify_pragma_suppresses

**Status:** merged (PR #268, 2026-04-18) ┬╖ **Order:** 30 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P4 ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** тАФ ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 55000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Added

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `pragma-directive-scope-manipulation` |
| Diagnosis | `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/SuppressionService.cs:42` emits `#pragma warning disable <id>` + `restore` pairs, but there is no tool for widening/moving pragma scope when the fire site is later than the initial placement. IT-Chat-Bot RetrievalExecutor bug: pragma pair wrapped line 68 but CA2025 actually fires at line 78. |
| Approach | Two complementary tools: (a) `verify_pragma_suppresses(filePath, line, diagnosticId)` тАФ check whether an existing pragma pair actually covers a diagnostic fire location; (b) `pragma_scope_widen(filePath, line, diagnosticId)` тАФ extend the matching `restore` past a specified statement. Both reuse `SuppressionService` primitives. |
| Scope | prod: 2 (`src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/SuppressionService.cs` extend with widen/verify, `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Tools/SuppressionTools.cs`). tests: +1 (`tests/RoslynMcp.Tests/PragmaScopeManipulationTests.cs`). |
| Risks | Widen must not cross `#region` boundaries or nest into other pragma-disable blocks incorrectly. Verify must correctly identify the fire site from the current diagnostic state (via `compile_check`). |
| Validation | Test: misaligned pragma pair (disable line 5, restore line 7, fire at line 10) тЖТ `verify_pragma_suppresses` returns false; `pragma_scope_widen` moves restore to line тЙе 10; second `verify` returns true. |
| Performance review | N/A. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Added:** `verify_pragma_suppresses` checks whether a pragma pair actually covers a diagnostic's fire site; `pragma_scope_widen` extends the `restore` past a given statement тАФ pair eliminates the class of cosmetic-pragma bugs (`pragma-directive-scope-manipulation`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `pragma-directive-scope-manipulation`. |

### 36. `concurrent-mcp-instances-no-tools` тАФ Parallel stdio host tool-registration race (heroic-last)

**Status:** pending ┬╖ **Order:** 36 ┬╖ **Correctness class:** P3-correctness ┬╖ **Schedule hint:** heroic-last ┬╖ **Estimated context:** 75000 tokens ┬╖ **CHANGELOG category:** Fixed

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Backlog rows closed | `concurrent-mcp-instances-no-tools` |
| Diagnosis | When multiple `roslynmcp` stdio instances start concurrently, subsequent instances report "server started" with an empty tool list. Root cause unknown per backlog; candidates include (a) stdio handle contention, (b) Claude Code MCP-host plugin-registration race, (c) shared resource (named pipe/socket/file lock), (d) deferred-tool schema load failing silently. Blocks multi-repo audits. |
| Approach | Investigation sequence, bounded at 4 hours of reading + 2 hours of patching: (1) add structured stderr logging in `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Program.cs` for init handshake + tool registration + tool list. (2) Reproduce in a controlled 4-process parallel launch. (3) Bisect root cause via stderr traces. (4) Ship the narrowest fix: either per-instance isolation (if shared resource), explicit init sequencing (if race), or loud failure (if currently silent). Heroic-last because step 3 may redirect the fix shape; do not ship before simpler wins. |
| Scope | prod: up to 3 (likely `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/Program.cs`, possibly `src/RoslynMcp.Roslyn/Services/WorkspaceManager.cs`, possibly a new `src/RoslynMcp.Host.Stdio/HostLogging.cs`). tests: +1 if the race is reproducible in a unit test, else a manual repro recipe in `docs/`. |
| Risks | Root cause may be in the Claude Code MCP host (not this repo), in which case ship diagnostics + file upstream. Fix of unknown shape тАФ budget may slip. Schedule LAST regardless of correctness class per Rule 5. |
| Validation | Manual: 4 concurrent `roslynmcp` subprocess launches; each reports correct tool list after init. Structured stderr shows no race. |
| Performance review | Diagnostics must not burden the hot path; log only during init. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Fixed:** Parallel `roslynmcp` stdio instances now register their full tool list reliably under concurrent launch тАФ closes the multi-repo-audit blocker (`concurrent-mcp-instances-no-tools`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `concurrent-mcp-instances-no-tools`. If investigation finds the root cause is in the MCP host, mark the row obsolete and file an upstream issue instead. |

| Validation | Scripted harness runs `fetch_mcp_resource` 100├Ч against each stable URI immediately after `workspace_load`; fail-rate тЙд 0%. |
| Performance review | Readiness gate must remain fast тАФ any added check should be <10ms. |
| CHANGELOG entry (draft) | **Fixed:** `fetch_mcp_resource` reliably returns the target resource after `workspace_load` completes тАФ closes the intermittent "server not ready" reports from Cursor (`roslyn-fetch-resource-timing`). |
| Backlog sync | Close rows: `roslyn-fetch-resource-timing`. If investigation finds the root cause is in the MCP client, mark obsolete and file an upstream issue. |
